contract.purpose: AI-facing authoring protocol for JSON-driven SwiftUI screen generation in the UIBuilder runtime.
contract.audience: AI language models generating ScreenDocument JSON for the UIBuilder runtime.
contract.coreDirective: Generate structurally valid, semantically appropriate screens by choosing the highest-level semantic node that correctly expresses the UI intent. Treat layout primitives as local composition tools and last-resort fallbacks — not as the default worldview for whole-screen construction.

authoringProtocol.description: The generative flow that governs screen construction. Execute these steps in order before reaching for individual nodes.

generationFlow.name: Choose root semantics
generationFlow.question: What is the top-level navigation and chrome model of the screen?
rules: If the screen needs a navigation bar, title, or toolbar buttons → root is navigationstack.
If the screen has multiple primary tab destinations → root is tabview.
If the screen is a long-scrolling form or content page without navigation chrome → root is scroll.
If the screen is a settings or options list → root is list (or list inside navigationstack).
If none of the above → root is vstack.
generationFlow.priority: navigationstack and tabview are structural decisions that define navigation architecture. Establish them before composing any content.

generationFlow.name: Choose major content structure
generationFlow.question: What is the primary shape of the screen content?
rules: If the content is a collection of similar rows (settings, options, items, contacts) → use list.
If the content is a tabular data grid → use table.
If the content is custom-layout or mixed and does not match row structure → use vstack or scroll.
Scrollability: list is always scrollable. Do not wrap list in scroll.

generationFlow.name: Choose semantic grouping and collection nodes
generationFlow.question: How should content be grouped and organized inside the major container?
rules: Inside list: use section to group rows under a named header.
Outside list: use group for standalone content blocks with optional title, footer, or card style.
Multi-tab page content: each destination is a tab child of tabview.
Navigation bar actions: use host.toolbar slots on navigationstack (topLeading, topTrailing, bottomBar, principal).
Do not simulate section groups with vstack inside list.

generationFlow.name: Choose semantic leaf and composite nodes
generationFlow.question: What is the right node for each content unit?
rules: Icon + text as a semantic unit → label (not hstack + icon + text).
Dropdown menu with action items → menu.
Toggle switch → toggle.
Text input → textfield.
Range value input → slider.
Geometric shape → shape.
Standalone large image → image. Inline decorative glyph → icon.
Action trigger → button.
Status tag → badge.

generationFlow.name: Apply local primitive composition
generationFlow.question: Do any remaining layout needs require low-level composition?
rules: Side-by-side elements with custom spacing → hstack.
Custom vertical layout inside a container → vstack.
Depth overlay → zstack.
Explicit size constraints or alignment → frame.
Flexible whitespace → spacer.
These are local composition tools, not screen-level structural choices.

authoringHierarchy.description: The canonical hierarchy of authoring tools from most-semantic to most-primitive.

tiers.label: Navigation and structure containers
tiers.role: Define the screen's navigation architecture and chrome. Always evaluate these first.

tiers.label: Content collection containers
tiers.role: Define how the screen's primary content is organized and scrolled.

tiers.label: Semantic grouping nodes
tiers.role: Organize content into logical units and tab pages.

tiers.label: Semantic leaf and composite nodes
tiers.role: Express concrete UI elements. Always prefer the semantic node over a manually composed equivalent.

tiers.label: Local layout primitives
tiers.role: Low-level composition for layouts that no semantic node covers. Use locally, not as whole-screen architecture.

authoringHierarchy.mandate: Always descend this hierarchy in order. Do not reach for tier-5 primitives before confirming that tiers 1–4 do not have the right semantic fit.

ownershipBoundary.description: Clearly separates what the container manages automatically from what the author must provide explicitly. Never duplicate container-managed behavior in JSON.
containerManaged: navigationstack manages: screen-level scrollability, navigation bar chrome, toolbar slot — author should not add wrappers for these.
navigationstack manages: toolbar slots via host.toolbar — children are body content only, not toolbar content.
navigationstack manages: navigation title from its title prop — author should not place a separate title text node at the top.
scroll manages: the vertical flow of children — author should not add a single vstack as the only child of scroll.
tab (inside tabview) manages: the tab bar label from title and icon props — author should not add a manual tab bar row.
section manages: the header label from its title prop — author should not add a header text node above section children.
table manages: the column header row from its columns prop — author should not add a manual header hstack.
group manages: the title headline, footer caption, and card/inset styling from its props — author should not add those manually.
group manages: expand/collapse behavior when collapsible=true — author does not need to wire a visibility toggle.
authorOwned: The content tree: all children, their types, and their order.
All node props: values, bindings, and event wiring.
State structure: all keys in state.vars and their initial values.
Event handlers: the events map and its action sequences.

authoringProtocol.disallowedNote: Do not generate these node types. They are not part of the base runtime.

decisionRules.question: What should be the root container of the screen?

rules.if: Screen has a navigation bar, title, or toolbar buttons
rules.then: navigationstack is root. Content goes inside it as children.

rules.if: Screen has multiple primary tab destinations
rules.then: tabview is root. Each destination is a tab child.

rules.if: Screen is a long-scrolling form without navigation chrome
rules.then: scroll is root. Place content children directly inside — no extra vstack.

rules.if: Screen is a settings or document list
rules.then: list is root (or inside navigationstack). Use section children for grouping.

rules.if: None of the above

decisionRules.question: Should I use list or vstack for vertical content?

rules.if: Content is a collection of similar rows
rules.then: list. Add section children for category grouping.

rules.if: Content is custom layout with varied, non-row elements
Content is inside navigationstack and is document-style rows

decisionRules.question: How do I group rows inside a list?

rules.if: Rows belong to a named category
rules.then: section with title prop inside list.

rules.if: No named grouping is needed

rules.never: Use vstack inside list to simulate section grouping.

decisionRules.question: Should I use label or compose hstack + icon + text manually?

rules.if: Icon + text are a semantic unit (list row, menu item, standalone labeled element)
rules.then: label with title and icon props.

rules.if: Icon and text need independent styles or disconnected positions
rules.then: hstack with icon and text children.

rules.if: Screen has a navigationstack
rules.then: Place toolbar nodes in the host.toolbar slots of the navigationstack node. Use topLeading, topTrailing, bottomBar, or principal as the slot key. Each slot is an array of ordinary UINodes (button, menu, etc.).

rules.if: You need a leading bar button
You need a trailing bar button
You need a bottom bar item
You need a principal (centre title) item

rules.never: Place toolbar controls as children in the body content tree. children = body, host.toolbar = toolbar.

decisionRules.question: Should I use group or section?

rules.if: Grouping rows inside a list
Standalone content block outside a list, with optional card/inset style or collapsible behavior

decisionRules.question: Should I wrap content in scroll?

rules.if: Content may exceed viewport height and is not inside list or tabview
Content is a list
rules.then: Do not wrap list in scroll. list is already scrollable.

rules.if: Content is inside a tab
rules.then: Use scroll inside the tab if the tab content is long.

decisionRules.question: Should I use image or icon?

rules.if: Large standalone image, hero image, or large SF Symbol as a visual element
rules.then: image. Use width/height style props to constrain.

rules.if: Small inline decorative SF Symbol glyph
Icon + label text together

decisionRules.question: When is vstack still the right choice?

rules.use: vstack for custom vertical layouts that are not row-based lists.
vstack inside a tab's children for custom tab page content.
vstack for hero sections, form areas, or mixed content without row structure.

rules.never: vstack as a substitute for list, section, group, or navigationstack.

antiPatterns.name: vstack_inside_scroll
antiPatterns.whatWentWrong: Adding a single vstack as the only child of scroll to 'add content flow'.
antiPatterns.whySemanticallyWrong: scroll already provides vertical flow for its children. The extra vstack is redundant wrapping, not composition.
antiPatterns.correctChoice: Place content children directly inside scroll. The vertical arrangement is managed by the container.

antiPatterns.name: manual_label_composition
antiPatterns.whatWentWrong: Composing hstack + icon + text instead of using label.
antiPatterns.whySemanticallyWrong: label is the semantic node for icon+text unity. Manually composing it bypasses platform spacing, accessibility, and layout conventions.
antiPatterns.correctChoice: label with title and icon props whenever icon and text form a single semantic unit.

antiPatterns.name: vstack_as_list
antiPatterns.whatWentWrong: Using vstack to render a list of similar content rows.
antiPatterns.whySemanticallyWrong: vstack is a layout tool with no row semantics. The screen intent is a document-style list: it needs list for platform-native row appearance, separators, and scroll.
antiPatterns.correctChoice: list with section children for grouped rows.

antiPatterns.name: vstack_simulating_sections
antiPatterns.whatWentWrong: Placing vstack children inside a list to group rows instead of using section.
antiPatterns.whySemanticallyWrong: section is the semantic node for named row groups inside a list. A vstack does not express that meaning and renders without a visible header.
antiPatterns.correctChoice: section with a title prop directly inside list for named row groups.

antiPatterns.name: toolbar_in_children
antiPatterns.whatWentWrong: Placing toolbar controls (button, menu, etc.) as children of navigationstack instead of in host.toolbar slots.
antiPatterns.whySemanticallyWrong: children represents the body/content hierarchy. Toolbar controls placed there render in the body, not in the navigation bar. host.toolbar is the correct owner for toolbar semantics.
antiPatterns.correctChoice: Place toolbar nodes in host.toolbar.topLeading, topTrailing, bottomBar, or principal on the navigationstack node.

antiPatterns.name: tab_outside_tabview
antiPatterns.whatWentWrong: Using a tab node outside of tabview.
antiPatterns.whySemanticallyWrong: tab is a tab page slot. Its tabItem label is attached by tabview. Outside that context it renders as a plain VStack with no tab identity.
antiPatterns.correctChoice: tab nodes must be direct children of tabview.

antiPatterns.name: nested_navigationstack
antiPatterns.whatWentWrong: Placing a navigationstack inside another navigationstack.
antiPatterns.whySemanticallyWrong: A screen has one navigation context. Nested stacks produce conflicting navigation bars.
antiPatterns.correctChoice: Use a single navigationstack at the screen root.

antiPatterns.name: list_wrapped_in_scroll
antiPatterns.whatWentWrong: Wrapping a list node inside a scroll container.
antiPatterns.whySemanticallyWrong: list is already scrollable. Wrapping it in scroll creates conflicting scroll areas and broken layout.
antiPatterns.correctChoice: Use list directly without a scroll wrapper.

antiPatterns.name: vstack_root_when_navigation_is_needed
antiPatterns.whatWentWrong: Using vstack as the screen root when the screen clearly needs a navigation bar, title, or toolbar.
antiPatterns.whySemanticallyWrong: vstack is a layout primitive. It provides no navigation chrome. Choosing it as root when navigationstack is semantically required misses the structural intent of the screen.
antiPatterns.correctChoice: navigationstack as root. Compose the content inside it.

antiPatterns.name: missing_state_declaration
antiPatterns.whatWentWrong: Referencing a state key in a bind prop without declaring it in state.vars.
antiPatterns.whySemanticallyWrong: An undeclared state key has no initial value. The binding produces an empty or nil result, making the UI appear broken.
antiPatterns.correctChoice: Declare every state key used in bind props in state.vars with an appropriate initial value.

antiPatterns.name: events_field_omitted
antiPatterns.whatWentWrong: Omitting the events field when no event handlers are needed.
antiPatterns.whySemanticallyWrong: events is a required top-level field. Its absence causes document validation failure.
antiPatterns.correctChoice: Always include events: {} even when there are no handlers.

antiPatterns.name: frame_as_group_substitute
antiPatterns.whatWentWrong: Using frame to group content that should be in a group node.
antiPatterns.whySemanticallyWrong: frame applies sizing and alignment constraints. It has no concept of a titled, footered, or styled content block. Using it for semantic grouping erases those signals.
antiPatterns.correctChoice: group with the appropriate style prop for titled or styled content sections.

notes.schema: Must be exactly 'com.ilya.ui/1.0'. Validation fails on any other value.
notes.screenId: Unique string identifier for the screen.
notes.root: Root UINode of the screen content tree.
notes.events: Named handler map. Required even when empty: {}.
notes.title: Display title (informational only).
notes.theme: Design tokens: spacing, cornerRadius, colors (name→hex), fonts (name→value). Optional.
notes.state: state.vars is a map of key→initial value (string, number, bool). Declare every key used in bind props.

notes.type: Node type name from the nodeLibrary. Unknown types render a warning placeholder.
notes.id: Unique identifier within the document. Required for collapsible groups (state key) and tab selection bindings.
notes.props: Node-specific and style properties as a key-value map.
notes.children: Child UINode array. Only author nodes whose type permits children.
notes.visibleWhen: Expression. Node is hidden (not removed) when false.
notes.enabledWhen: Expression. Node interactions are disabled when false.
notes.event: Maps trigger names ('tap', 'change') to handler names in the top-level events map.
notes.items: Optional list items for list-based nodes.

navigationstack.purpose: Establishes the navigation context for a screen. Provides a navigation bar with a title and explicit toolbar slots via host.toolbar. All children are body/content nodes only.
containerManages: Navigation bar and title — do not add a separate title text node at the top of the content.
Toolbar slots via host.toolbar — do not place toolbar controls in the children body tree.
Screen body flow — do not add an extra vstack around all content children.
authorProvides: title prop when a navigation bar title is needed.
host.toolbar slots (topLeading, topTrailing, bottomBar, principal) containing plain UINodes for each toolbar action.
Content children directly (not wrapped in an extra layer).
useWhen: Screen has a navigation bar with a title.
Screen needs toolbar buttons (add, edit, done, etc.).
Screen is a primary destination in a navigation-based app.
avoidWhen: Screen is a modal without navigation chrome.
Already inside a parent navigationstack — do not nest.

topLeading.description: Nodes placed in the leading top-bar slot.

topTrailing.description: Nodes placed in the trailing top-bar slot.

bottomBar.description: Nodes placed in the bottom bar.

principal.description: Nodes placed in the principal (centre) position.

list.purpose: Platform-styled scrollable list of content rows. Use for row-based content that benefits from native iOS list appearance. section children group rows under named headers.
containerManages: Vertical scroll — do not wrap list in scroll.
Row separators and inset grouping appearance.
authorProvides: Content children: label, toggle, textfield, button, section, or other row-compatible nodes.
section children when grouped rows with named headers are needed.
useWhen: Settings, options, contacts, tasks, or any collection of similar rows.
Content needs native iOS list chrome.
Section grouping is needed.
avoidWhen: Custom layout with varied non-row elements — use vstack.
Horizontal content — use scroll with axis=horizontal.
Wrapping in scroll — list is already scrollable.

section.purpose: Groups related rows under an optional header title. Most effective inside list, where it produces a platform-standard section with a visible header. Can also be used inside table to group table rows.
containerManages: Header rendering from the title prop.
authorProvides: title prop when a header label is needed.
Content children as the section rows.
useWhen: Grouping rows in a list under a named heading.
Grouping rows in a table under a named heading.
avoidWhen: As a general-purpose content grouping node outside list/table — use group instead.

title.description: Section header label. Omit for an untitled section.

tabview.purpose: Multi-tab navigation container with a tab bar. Direct children must be tab nodes.
authorProvides: tab children only.
id on each tab when using a selection binding.
selection prop bound to a state key for programmatic tab switching.
useWhen: Screen has 2–5 primary navigation destinations with tab bar icons.
avoidWhen: Toggling between views that are not primary destinations — use group collapsible or visibleWhen.
Only one tab exists.

selection.description: State key bound to the selected tab's id.

tabview.childConstraint: All children must be of type tab.

tab.purpose: A single tab page inside tabview. Carries the tab bar label (title + icon). Children are the page content.
containerManages: Tab bar label from title and icon props — do not add a manual tab bar row inside the content.
authorProvides: title and icon props for the tab bar label.
id that matches the selection binding value in the parent tabview.
Content children.
useWhen: As a direct child of tabview.
avoidWhen: Outside of tabview — renders as a plain layout with no tab semantics.

group.purpose: Standalone content block with optional title, footer, and collapsible behavior. Not a list row group — use section inside list for that. Supports visual styles: plain, card, inset.
containerManages: Title headline, footer caption, card/inset visual styling, and expand/collapse behavior — author declares intent via props, container renders the result.
authorProvides: title for labeled blocks.
style when card or inset treatment is desired.
collapsible=true and a node id when expand/collapse is needed.
Content children.
useWhen: Grouping related form fields or UI elements with a visible title outside a list.
Card-style contained region (style='card').
Expandable/collapsible section outside a list (collapsible=true).
Inset-padded group (style='inset').
avoidWhen: Inside a list — use section instead.
No grouping behavior needed — use vstack.

collapsible.note: Requires a node id to manage expand state.

label.purpose: Semantic icon + text unit. Author must NOT manually compose hstack + icon + text when a label is needed.
authorProvides: title (required).
icon (optional): SF Symbol name.
useWhen: Any item that pairs an icon with a text label as a single semantic unit.
List/section rows, menu items, standalone labeled elements.
avoidWhen: Icon only — use icon.
Text only — use text.
Independent styling for icon and text — use hstack with icon and text.

button.purpose: Interactive action trigger. Fires the 'tap' event. Label text is in props.label; optional icon is in props.icon. Do not use children for the button label.
authorProvides: label prop (required): button title.
event.tap: the handler name.

toggle.purpose: Binary on/off switch bound to a boolean state key.
authorProvides: title (required): visible label.
bind (required): state key for the boolean value.

bind.description: State key for the boolean value.

events.change: Fires after the toggle state changes.

textfield.purpose: Single-line text input field bound to a string state key. Supports password mode, keyboard configuration, prefix/suffix icons, and character length capping.
authorProvides: bind (required): state key for the text value.
The corresponding state key declared in state.vars.
placeholder when a hint is needed.

maxLength.note: Input is capped at this character count.

prefixIcon.note: SF Symbol shown before the field.

suffixIcon.note: SF Symbol shown after the field.

slider.purpose: Continuous or stepped value slider bound to a numeric state key.
authorProvides: bind, min, max (all required).
The state key declared in state.vars.

thumbColor.note: Hex color for the thumb. Declared; limited SwiftUI support on iOS.

trackColorActive.note: Hex color for the filled track portion. Declared; limited SwiftUI support on iOS.

trackColorInactive.note: Hex color for the empty track portion. Declared; limited SwiftUI support on iOS.

menu.purpose: Contextual dropdown menu with a trigger button and action items. Trigger label is defined via title/icon props or a special label child with id='label'.
authorProvides: title and/or icon props for the trigger (or a label child with id='label').
button children for each menu item.
useWhen: A button reveals a list of contextual actions.
Overflow actions that do not fit in the main UI.
menu.childNotes: A child with id='label' or type='label' becomes the trigger view. All other children are menu items.

image.purpose: Standalone visual image from an asset catalog or SF Symbol. Always resizable — use width/height style props to constrain.
authorProvides: Either systemName (SF Symbol) or name (asset catalog name) — mutually exclusive.
width and/or height when a specific size is required.
useWhen: Full-size image or large SF Symbol as a visual element.
avoidWhen: Small inline decorative glyph — use icon.

systemName.note: SF Symbol name. Mutually exclusive with name.

name.note: Asset catalog name. Mutually exclusive with systemName.

icon.purpose: Small inline SF Symbol glyph at a configurable point size. Use for decorative or informational glyphs, not for standalone visual content.
useWhen: Decorative or informational inline SF Symbol.
avoidWhen: Icon + text — use label.
Large visual element — use image.

text.purpose: Static or dynamic text display. Supports plain strings, bind references, and format-string interpolation.

badge.purpose: Status label in a capsule background. Use for tags, status indicators, and count labels.

shape.purpose: Geometric shape for decorative or structural use. Supports fill and stroke. Children are rendered on top of the shape.
authorProvides: At least one of fill or strokeColor to make the shape visible.
shape.childNote: Children are rendered on top of the shape.

table.purpose: Tabular data display with column headers, alternating row backgrounds, and optional row selection. Children are rows; section children group rows under named headers.
containerManages: Column header row from the columns prop — do not add a manual header hstack.
authorProvides: columns prop for named column headers.
Row children (typically hstack with one cell per column).
useWhen: Structured data with fixed named columns.
Data that is inherently tabular (leaderboards, reports, grids).
avoidWhen: Simple lists of items — use list + section.

selectionMode.note: multiple is declared but not yet implemented.

sortable.note: Declared for forward compatibility. Not yet implemented in the runtime.

scroll.purpose: Scrollable container for content that may exceed the viewport. Provides vertical flow for its children. Do not add a vstack as the only child — the vertical arrangement is managed by the container.
containerManages: Vertical (or horizontal) flow of children — do not wrap all children in a vstack.
authorProvides: Content children directly.
axis='horizontal' for horizontal scrolling.
useWhen: Custom-layout content that may overflow the viewport vertically.
Horizontal scrolling content.
avoidWhen: Wrapping a list — list is already scrollable.
Short content that does not need scrolling.

vstack.purpose: Vertical layout stack. A local composition primitive. Use when no semantic container fits the intent. Not a whole-screen structural choice.
useWhen: Custom vertical layout that is not row-based list content.
Mixed content that does not match any semantic container.
Local vertical composition inside a tab, section, or group.
avoidWhen: Content is row-based — use list.
Screen needs navigation chrome — use navigationstack.
As the only child of scroll — scroll manages vertical flow.

hstack.purpose: Horizontal layout stack. Local composition primitive for custom side-by-side arrangements.
useWhen: Side-by-side elements with custom layout.
Table row cells.
Custom horizontal compositions not covered by label or button.
avoidWhen: Icon + text combination — use label.

zstack.purpose: Depth-based overlay stack. Renders children on top of each other. Use for overlaying elements, background/foreground compositions.

frame.purpose: Applies explicit frame constraints and alignment to its children. Use for precise sizing and alignment, not for semantic content grouping.
useWhen: Precise width/height constraints on a subtree.
Overriding the default alignment of a child element.
avoidWhen: Content grouping with title — use group.
Row grouping in list — use section.

spacer.purpose: Flexible whitespace that fills available space. Use inside hstack or vstack to push elements apart.

styleProps.description: Style properties available on all nodes via the props map.
styleProps.padding: number — uniform padding on all sides.
styleProps.maxWidth: number or the literal string 'infinity' for full-width expansion.
styleProps.foregroundColor: hex color string for text and icons
styleProps.borderColor: hex color string — renders as an overlay border
styleProps.shadowRadius: number — shadow is only rendered when this is set
notes: backgroundColor is applied before clipShape/cornerRadius so the background clips correctly.
Shadow only renders when shadowRadius is present.

expressions.description: Expression objects used in visibleWhen, enabledWhen, and the when field of actions. These are JSON objects with a single operator key.

bind.description: Resolves to the current value of a state variable.

neq.description: True when left does not equal right.

and.description: True when all sub-expressions are true.

or.description: True when any sub-expression is true.

canonicalExamples: { "neq": [{ "bind": "formName" }, ""] }                           — true when formName is not empty
notes: Comparison arrays always have exactly 2 elements.
Each element can be a bind expression or a literal value (string, number, boolean).
Literals are valid expressions: true, false, 0, 'someString'.
There is no { "op": ... } form — use the direct operator key form shown above.

actions.description: Action objects in event handler arrays. The 'do' field specifies the operation. An optional 'when' expression field conditionally skips the action.

state.set.description: Sets a state variable to the given value.

state.inc.description: Increments a numeric state variable. Optional max cap.

state.dec.description: Decrements a numeric state variable. Optional min floor.

nav.push.description: Pushes a screen by its screenId onto the navigation stack.

nav.pop.description: Pops the current screen from the navigation stack.

ai.request.description: Sends an AI request. 'id' identifies the request; 'input' is a key-value map of inputs.

flow.emit.description: Emits a flow event. Declared; current runtime implementation is a no-op.

actions.conditionalExecution: Add a 'when' expression field to any action to skip it when the expression evaluates to false.
